page: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd5 is a bare metal server configured with an AMD EPYC 9255 processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU features 48 physical cores and 48 dedicated vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 960 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in several areas. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the top 25%, demonstrating strong parallel processing capabilities. Memory performance is highly competitive, with PassMark memory scores placing in the top 10% and read-only memory bandwidth tests reaching the top 25%. However, large-block memory write bandwidth is average, placing in the middle 50%.

The server demonstrates high efficiency for memory-intensive and compute-heavy workloads. It achieves top-tier throughput in static web serving and small-model LLM inference, alongside strong database operation and Redis SET performance. This hardware profile delivers excellent resource density and high-performance throughput without the virtualization overhead of standard cloud instances. It is highly suitable for memory-optimized databases, high-throughput web serving, and localized machine learning inference tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd5 is a bare metal server featuring a 48-core AMD EPYC 9255 processor running at 3.2 GHz and 384.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server includes a 960 GB NVMe SSD and delivers dedicated, non-virtualized compute resources. Benchmark results place its single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory score in the top 10%, while multi-core execution and database operations rank in the top 25%. Although large-block memory write speeds are average, the platform excels in high-throughput scenarios, including static web serving and small-model LLM inference. This server is optimized for memory-intensive databases, data processing, and high-concurrency web applications.
og_description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd5 is a bare metal server featuring a 48-core AMD EPYC 9255 CPU, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory and single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-24c-384gb-amd5 is a bare metal server with a 48-core AMD EPYC CPU, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 960 GB NVMe storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining forty-eight AMD EPYC cores with high-speed DDR5 memory and NVMe storage for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9255 processor with 48 physical cores and 3.2 GHz clock speed
384.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
960 GB local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency data access
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory benchmark scores
Well-suited for memory-intensive databases, static web serving, and LLM inference
